programme.desc: The bodies of several young women began turning up in shallow graves in the Alaskan wilderness. In one of the first cases of successful criminal investigative analysis, FBI profilers mapped the criminal's mind and captured the serial killer.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, the FBI and the NYPD began a joint investigation into gas station mogul Gurmeet Dhinsa for gas pump rigging. However, they soon realized Dhinsa ruled his mob-like operation with brutal force, kidnapping and killing wayward employees.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1990, the body of a suspected drug courier was discovered burning in a Minnesota alleyway. The FBI teamed up with local police to uncover a dangerous crime ring run by a drug lord who used violence and intimidation to keep those around him quiet.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1993, a mother, her 8-year-old son and a live-in nurse were brutally murdered by an assassin. The murder-for-hire plot was meticulously planned and executed.

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The bodies of several women began turning up around Vienna, Austria. The FBI and Austrian Federal Police investigated suspected serial killer Jack Unterweger, who became an international outlaw, hopping from country to country to evade capture.

programme.desc: In 1985, South Carolina serial killer Larry Gene Bell was a predator, abducting girls and young women.

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.desc: A master manipulator accused of the murder of his wife eventually caught out by a polygraph test - and his own fiancée.
